title: The lawyer who woke up to three missed inquiries — and zero contact details to follow up with

cta.text: Create your AI assistant — let your website answer while you're asleep.

package_brief.hook: Someone typed an urgent question into your website last night

video.script:
BEAT 1 — HOOK (0–3s): Tight close on a person's hands holding a phone in dim evening light, typing an urgent question into a website. The message sends. A 'seen' indicator appears. No reply. The screen sits quiet.

BEAT 2 — SETUP (3–10s): Cut to a home kitchen, cool morning light. A professional — lawyer, consultant, service owner — stands at the counter with coffee, scrolling their phone. Inbox: empty. Analytics: traffic spike from last night. The gap between those two facts hangs in the air.

BEAT 3 — ESCALATION (10–17s): We see the visitor's side again — three separate chat threads, all sent, all unanswered. No email, no name, no contact detail left behind. Each thread fades to grey. The visitor is already gone. The business owner has nothing to follow up with.

BEAT 4 — RESOLUTION (17–24s): Same late-night scene. Same urgent question typed. But this time, an AI response appears instantly — calm, helpful, on-brand. The visitor replies. A name and email are captured. Cut to the owner's morning kitchen — this time, a notification in the inbox. One name. One lead. Caught while they slept.

VOICEOVER runs across all beats. CTA delivered in final seconds.
video.concept: Handheld urgency: the camera opens tight on a person's hands typing an urgent question into a website chat on their phone, late at night. The reply thread shows 'seen' — and nothing more. We widen to reveal the business owner's side: a morning kitchen scene, coffee in hand, checking an empty inbox. The irony lands hard — the website had visitors, the owner had no idea. We escalate: multiple missed threads, zero contact details, no way back. Then the resolution: the same website, same late-night visitor — but this time a calm AI response appears instantly. The visitor stays. The lead is captured. The owner wakes up to a name in their inbox.

package_brief.scenario: A potential client lands on a law firm's website at 11 PM to ask about a contract dispute, but there's no one available to respond and they leave without leaving any contact details.
package_brief.subtitles: Urgent question dies in silence. | Someone typed into your website last night. Hit send. And waited. | Your site said nothing back. | No answer. No form. No way to reach them this morning. | They moved on. You never knew they were there. | Every unanswered visitor is a lead you can't chase — you never got their name. | Your website was open. You just weren't in it. | Fenrik.chat answers for you. | Create your AI assistant today.
image_prompts: Soft polished 3D render, portrait 9:16 vertical frame. Home kitchen environment, cool clear morning light. Three-quarter angle. Centered subject with generous vertical headroom. Close on a person's hands holding a smartphone in dim evening-toned light — the phone screen glows softly, showing a chat interface with a sent message bubble and a small 'seen' indicator beneath it. No reply bubble. The screen is quiet. The hands are still, waiting. Natural textures, restrained saturation, soft pastel accents. No readable text, no UI labels, no notifications visible as text. The emotional state is tension — a question sent into silence.

Soft polished 3D render, portrait 9:16 vertical frame. Home kitchen with everyday realism, cool clear morning light from above-left, soft pastel accents, restrained saturation. Three-quarter angle on a professional figure — gender-neutral, business-casual — standing at a kitchen counter holding a coffee cup in one hand, scrolling a phone with the other. Their expression is mildly puzzled, searching. The phone screen shows a soft glow suggesting an analytics or inbox view — no readable text. A sense of quiet unease: something happened last night and the inbox is empty. Two people at conversational distance not posed — a second figure visible softly blurred in background, going about their morning. Centered subject, generous vertical headroom.

Soft polished 3D render, portrait 9:16 vertical frame. Home kitchen environment, cool clear morning light. Three-quarter angle. The scene shows a person's hand holding a phone, screen facing the viewer — three faded chat thread previews visible as abstract rounded shapes, no readable text, each progressively greyed out suggesting unanswered conversations. The emotional tone is loss — opportunities that dissolved. Soft pastel accents, natural kitchen textures in background. No labels, no metrics, no visible words. The visual meaning: multiple visitors came, asked, and left without a trace.

Soft polished 3D render, portrait 9:16 vertical frame. Home kitchen environment, cool clear morning light. Three-quarter angle on a professional figure at the kitchen counter, same setting as beat 2. This time their expression is calm and quietly pleased — they are looking at their phone with a small smile. The phone screen glows with a soft notification indicator, abstract shape suggesting a new message received. A sense of relief and quiet optimism. Soft pastel accents, restrained saturation, centered subject with generous vertical headroom. The emotional shift: something was captured while they slept. No readable text, no UI labels.

package_brief.voiceover_text: Someone typed an urgent question into your website last night. Hit send. And waited. Your site said nothing back. No answer, no form, no way to reach them this morning. They moved on. You never even knew they were there. Every unanswered visitor is a lead you can't chase — because you never got their name. Your website was open. You just weren't in it. Fenrik.chat answers for you. Create your AI assistant today.

x.cta: Create your AI assistant at fenrik.chat
x.caption: Your website had visitors last night. Your inbox has zero of them. That's not a quiet night — that's a lead gap you can't close.
title_variants: Traffic up. Inbox empty. The math doesn't add up.
The lead that never left a name
Being 'open' online means nothing if your site can't answer
Three visitors. Zero contact details. One avoidable problem.
Your website is getting questions it's not allowed to answer
caption_variants: Your website had visitors last night. Your inbox has zero of them. That's not a quiet night — that's a lead gap you can't close.
Someone typed an urgent question into your site at 11 PM. Saw no response. Left. You woke up to an empty inbox and a traffic spike that means nothing now.
Being available online isn't the same as being responsive. Your website is open 24/7. Your answers aren't. That's the gap.
Three people visited a law firm's site after hours last week. All three had real questions. None left contact details. All three moved on. This is fixable.
Your contact form isn't availability. It's a polite way of saying 'come back later.' Most visitors won't.

facebook.cta: Create your AI assistant at fenrik.chat
facebook.caption: Ever open your inbox in the morning and find... nothing — even though your analytics show people visited your website overnight? 😬 That's not a quiet night. That's missed leads with no way to follow up. An AI assistant on your website answers visitors the moment they ask — even at 11 PM on a Tuesday. No staff needed, no coding, no setup headache. Create yours at fenrik.chat and see it live before you commit.

linkedin.cta: Create your AI assistant at fenrik.chat
linkedin.caption: Here's a pattern worth paying attention to: a professional services website gets real traffic overnight — a lawyer, a consultant, an accountant — but the inbox is empty every morning. Not because nobody came. Because nobody answered. Visitors with genuine intent don't wait. They move to the next result. The gap isn't marketing. It's response time outside business hours. An AI assistant on your website closes that gap without adding headcount.
title_variants: The silent cost of an empty inbox after a traffic spike
Why professional services websites lose leads while the owner sleeps
caption_variants: Here's a pattern worth paying attention to: a professional services website gets real traffic overnight — a lawyer, a consultant, an accountant — but the inbox is empty every morning. Not because nobody came. Because nobody answered. Visitors with genuine intent don't wait. They move to the next result. The gap isn't marketing. It's response time outside business hours. An AI assistant on your website closes that gap without adding headcount.

Most professional services websites have the same quiet problem: they attract qualified visitors after hours and have no mechanism to respond. No chat, no instant answer, no captured contact. The visitor leaves. The owner never knew. It's not a traffic problem — it's a response problem. And it's one an AI assistant can solve without a developer, a new hire, or a complex integration.

instagram.cta: Create your AI assistant — link in bio.
instagram.caption:
They came to your website at 11 PM with a real question.

Your site had no answer. No chat. No form they bothered to fill.

By morning, they were already somewhere else — and you had no idea they'd even shown up.

This is what 'we'll get back to you' actually costs.

opening.first_spoken_guidance: Open with an immediate reaction using Irony — not context or setup. The first spoken thought should land in ~1.0–1.8 seconds (one short phrase, or two very short phrases). The stored hook MUST be the same thought as the first spoken line — do not dilute a strong hook into a weaker voiceover setup. Lead with the ironic mismatch between what people claim and what they do. Narrative seed: Unexpected but relevant: Lead with the ironic mismatch between what people claim and what they do. Keep the link to Walk through the exact moment a small business owner realizes their website was visited overnight by ready-to-hire prospects who left no trace — exposing the silent cost of having no way to respond to visitors outside business hours. The story centers on a professional services owner checking their analytics in the morning and seeing the traffic spike, but finding an empty inbox. No names, no emails, no way back in. The pain point is visceral: you were open for business on paper, but completely closed in practice. / Unable to answer customer questions when offline clear by the next beat.
opening.first_visual_guidance: The first visual is an attention event, not a sentence illustration. Visual contradiction: appearance vs reality in one readable frame. Preferred opening visual concept: A productivity trophy collecting dust next to an overflowing unread ideas pile about Walk through the exact moment a small business owner realizes their website was visited overnight by ready-to-hire prospects who left no trace — exposing the silent cost of having no way to respond to visitors outside business hours. The story centers on a professional services owner checking their analytics in the morning and seeing the traffic spike, but finding an empty inbox. No names, no emails, no way back in. The pain point is visceral: you were open for business on paper, but completely closed in practice. Do not default to calm desks, empty boards, laptop+coffee, or faceless screen-staring unless that is genuinely the strongest idea. Render coherently via Visual Narrative / Medium / Profile / Identity — attention chooses the idea, those systems choose the look.
opening.first_subtitle_guidance: First subtitle mirrors the first spoken thought — same words, no softer paraphrase.

phases.delivery: Opening: bold, steady confidence on the first claim.
Body: conversational contrast — short sentences, not one long equally-paced paragraph.
Emphasis: slight lift on the key turn, contradiction, or insight line.
Brief pause before the reveal, punchline, or payoff.
Close: satisfying landing; CTA only if present, never aggressive.

delivery_arc.tts_instruction_fragment: Opening: bold, steady confidence on the first claim. Then settle into conversational body delivery. Use spoken rhythm: short sentences, contrast, and a brief pause before the reveal. Emphasize the turn or punchline — do not read every clause at the same energy. Avoid long equally-paced paragraphs; land one idea per breath. Dry, knowing, slightly amused.

presentation_generation.delivery_reason: Delivery: direct, empathetic, slightly frustrated. Delivery: thoughtful, reflective, steady pacing. Delivery: warm and approachable. Delivery: confident, concise, not aggressive. Language: en.

visual_narrative.key: human|body language that shows the relationship to the idea in one glance — not a device hero frame
visual_narrative.subject_focus: body language that shows the relationship to the idea in one glance — not a device hero frame
product_world_hints: Digital assistant world (meaning, not scenery): film unanswered visitors, people waiting for a reply, someone walking away, after-hours silence becoming answered — NOT automatic storefronts, NOT dashboards, NOT abstract boats/notebooks standing in for visitors.
Agency world: client conversations, missed follow-ups, collaborative tension — situations first, workshop props only when they are part of the event.
Product Brain constrains MEANING (who hurts, what changed), not scenery. Do not force browser UI, dashboards, or physical stores unless that situation is truly strongest.
visual_narrative.preferred_situation_framing: Situation first: film a person walking away unanswered / waiting for a reply — NOT a paper boat, closed notebook, or abstract prop standing in for the visitor.
